name: AvatarStack
description: Compact overlapping avatar group with optional overflow counter and inspectable hidden users.

props.name: size
props.description: Shared size for all avatars in the stack.

props.name: max
props.description: Maximum visible avatars; extra avatars are hidden and counted.

props.name: total
props.description: Total count; when greater than the visible count, renders a +N counter.

props.name: overflowUsers
props.description: Hidden users shown in the overflow list; each user supports name, optional secondaryText, src, and initials.

props.name: selectedIndex
props.description: Currently selected user index in the overflow list.

props.name: onCounterClick
props.description: Called when the +N counter button is clicked.

props.name: onOverflowUserClick
props.description: Called when an overflow user row is clicked.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional class name for the root avatar stack.

states.rationale: AvatarStack is passive except for the overflow counter. The counter uses OKLCH lightness shifts for hover and pressed states, and the shared focus ring for keyboard focus.

keyboard.Tab: Moves focus to the overflow counter and then to overflow rows when present.
keyboard.Enter/Space: Opens the focused counter or activates an overflow row through native button behavior.
keyboard.Escape: Closes the overflow list and returns focus to the counter.

propertyMapping.note: Visibility maps to providing the first Avatar child.
Visibility maps to providing the second Avatar child.
Visibility maps to providing the third Avatar child.
Visibility maps to providing the fourth Avatar child.
React derives counter visibility from max, total, and hidden child count.
The visible +N value is computed as total minus visible count.
Design interaction state. In React, use onRemove on the child Avatar.
Rows can include name, secondaryText, src, and initials.
React uses Avatar children instead of visibility booleans.
Derived from max, total, and hidden child count.
Maps to child Avatar onRemove behavior.

useWhen: Compact group identity for users, teams, accounts, or participants
Two or more overlapping avatars in a table, card, item, top bar, or summary region
A small visible group with a +N overflow count
A compact overflow list of hidden users
doNotUseFor: Large people lists or comparison workflows; use List or Table
Arbitrary icon clusters; use IconShape or a purpose-built control

knownDeviations.description: Figma exposes avatar1 through avatar4 visibility booleans, while React uses explicit Avatar children.
Figma exposes counter as a boolean, while React derives counter visibility and value from max, total, and hidden children.
Figma exposes hovered as a property. React models the removable hovered state through the child Avatar onRemove affordance instead of an AvatarStack prop.
